name: UNIV. OF DURHAM PSYCHOLOGY DEPT R1

surface_od_m.qualifier: absent — Ground Level box blank on all three sheets

units: metres, typed site-investigation log (Soils Engineering Services), transcribed verbatim

intervals.verbatim: Topsoil over sandy clay with coal traces.

annotations: continues from sheet 1 onto sheet 2; description re-typed 'Sand.' on the continuation sheet — one interval
thin bed: both bounding depths (25.30, 25.50) typed against a narrow band on sheet 2
continues from sheet 2 onto sheet 3; description re-typed 'Sand.' on the continuation sheet — one interval
pencil beside description: 0.45 (agrees with typed depths 47.60-48.05)
pencil in Legend column, faint, probable reading: 18" (0.45 m = 17.7 in, consistent as an imperial thickness note)
no seam name given anywhere on the sheet
typed below the interval, in the description column: 'Boring suspended.'

notes: Soils Engineering Services typed rotary log, borehole R1, Durham University, sheets 1-3 of 3 plus a location plan (page 4). YEAR DISCREPANCY: the BGS index gives 1986 but all three sheets read 'Drilling Commenced 21-26.3.1983' — 1983 recorded here, from the document. Header: 'Borehole drilled by rotary drilling techniques. SX size casing to 6.0m. PX size casing to 46.6m.' (sheet 1; the SX size figure is partly obscured by a punch-hole on every sheet). Ground Level box blank on all sheets; no Reduced Level or Standing Water Level entries anywhere; Samples/Tests columns empty; no water observations recorded. Handwritten header marginalia: 'NZ 24 SE/119' and 'MINERAL (NORTHERN) O.G.D. No. 08628'. Log ends 'Boring suspended.' at 51.15 m, matching the index depth. Page 4 is a 1:1250-style location plan showing R1 south-west of the Department of Psychology building, annotated in ink 'NZ 24 SE Approx N.G. Coords NZ 2772/4115' (consistent with the indexed grid reference 427715,541152); no strata content. The only pencil marginalia on the strata sheets are the two marks against the Coal interval (0.45 and probable 18"); no seam-name correlations anywhere, so seam_marks is empty. Depth arithmetic is internally consistent throughout (thicknesses derived from successive typed depths).
